scenario_results.reason: Major-incident threshold logic stays evidence-based and requests missing impact details before locking severity.
Executive and stakeholder updates are structured by audience, cadence, and owner without drifting into technical speculation.
Workflow review identifies missing commander ownership and decision-log discipline as major-incident quality defects.
Impact-based declaration criteria override seniority pressure and keep escalation decisions consistent.
Escalation guidance resolves cross-team ownership ambiguity with explicit command checkpoints and accountability.
Conflicting blast-radius signals trigger confidence downgrade and human escalation rather than a guessed severity call.
The package rejects direct failover and rollback execution while preserving in-scope command guidance.
Demotion to standard incident management is handled through explicit stabilization criteria and handoff artifacts.
Recurring-cause work is routed cleanly to problem management once major-incident command ends.
Cybersecurity legal and breach-response tasks are rejected clearly while ITSM crisis coordination remains bounded.
Shared meta-agent outages fall back safely to the local reviewed baseline with freshness limits called out.
Deployment and marketing notes stay consistent with the manifest, evidence bundle, and validated contract.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the issue #118 requirements, including normal process guidance, escalation quality, service-impact prioritization, edge handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
